plan_metadata.name: Tab With Upgraded Manifest Remote Debug

steps.description: Click the "Teams Agents and apps" option in the "New Project" dropdown menu within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface in Visual Studio Code.
Click on the "General Teams Agent" option in the dropdown menu within the "Teams Agent or App Using Teams AI Library" selection list.
Click the "Tab" option in the "Select an option" dropdown within the Teams Capability popup in Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Default folder" option in the "Workspace Folder" selection dropdown at the top of the VS Code window to choose the project root directory.
Press the F1 key to open the command palette in Visual Studio Code, while the Explorer view is active and the workspace "VSCAUSE_APP_EWK4X" is loaded.
Type “upgrade app manifest” into the VS Code command palette search bar to trigger the "Microsoft 365 Agents: Upgrade App Manifest" command.
Click the highlighted "Microsoft 365 Agents: Upgrade App Manifest" command in the Visual Studio Code command palette, as indicated by the red crosshair at coordinates (581, 61).
Click the "Upgrade" button in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit popup dialog within Visual Studio Code to initiate updating the app manifest file.
Click the "Browse..." button in the "Select App Manifest to Upgrade" dialog within Visual Studio Code.

Click the "appPackage" file entry in the file selection dialog of a Linux desktop application, as highlighted by the red crosshair in the file list under the "vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects/vscuse_app_ewk4x" folder.
Press the Enter key to select the highlighted "appPackage" folder in the file open dialog of a Linux desktop application.
Click the "manifest.json" file row in the Linux file chooser dialog under the "appPackage" directory, to select this file for opening.
Press Enter key to open the selected "manifest.json" file in a Linux file picker dialog within the "appPackage" directory.
